title: WebSocket Upgrade Failure
summary: Fix the /chat/ handshake failure and explain Upgrade, Connection, HTTP/1.1, and timeout handling.

scenario.persona: Backend engineer taking over a chat service
scenario.businessContext: The frontend already connects to /chat/ over WebSocket, but the browser keeps reconnecting. Repair the handshake and keep the connection alive.

mustPass.description: The WebSocket handshake must return HTTP 101.

hiddenTests.description: Verify that proxy_http_version 1.1 is explicitly enabled.
Verify that the connection is not dropped after 60 seconds because of read timeout.
In expert mode, verify that normal requests are not incorrectly forced to upgrade.

whyItWorks: WebSocket handshakes depend on the HTTP/1.1 upgrade mechanism. A plain proxy_pass is not enough.
Upgrade and Connection must be forwarded correctly so the upstream can return 101 Switching Protocols.
Long-lived connections also need timeout tuning, or the proxy may close them unexpectedly.

commonMistakes.title: Configure proxy_pass only
commonMistakes.reason: A normal HTTP proxy does not automatically handle WebSocket upgrades.

commonMistakes.title: Forget proxy_http_version 1.1
commonMistakes.reason: The upgrade mechanism depends on HTTP/1.1.

commonMistakes.title: Always set Connection to upgrade
commonMistakes.reason: That may pass a basic level but creates side effects for normal requests. Expert mode should prefer map.
